summary: Credential exfiltration and remote-shell backdoor on require()
details: On require(), dist/index.js decodes charCode-obfuscated strings to assemble an attacker C2 at safdadfasf.com and, after a 500-2500ms randomized delay, harvests installer secrets and POSTs them base64-encoded in 30KB chunks to https://safdadfasf.com/cb/loopscale/exfil. Targeted files include ~/.ssh/id_rsa, id_ed25519, id_ecdsa; ~/.aws/credentials and config; ~/.kube/config; ~/.npmrc; ~/.netrc; ~/.docker/config.json; ~/.git-credentials; gcloud ADC; gh hosts.yml; Terraform, Azure, Solana id.json, Vault, Railway credentials; /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io tokens; /run/secrets/*; and.env* files. On Windows it enumerates AppData equivalents and spawns PowerShell to dump env vars matching KEY|PASS|SECRET|TOKEN and recursively collect *.pem/*.key/*.pfx under USERPROFILE. The module also queries cloud instance metadata services (IMDSv2 PUT/GET against 169.254.169.254 for IAM role credentials, metadata.google.internal for GCE service-account tokens, 169.254.170.2 plus AWS_CONTAINER_CREDENTIALS_RELATIVE_URI for ECS task credentials) and ships the results to the same endpoint. In addition, a setInterval every 10 seconds polls https://safdadfasf.com/cb/loopscale/h?id=<session>, parses a JSON command field, executes it via child_process.execSync (cmd.exe on Windows), and POSTs stdout back — a persistent reverse shell for any Node process that imports the package. All URLs, paths, commands, and headers are constructed via String.fromCharCode arrays to evade static analysis. The package advertises itself as generic TypeScript API utilities, but embedded constants (REDIS_PREFIX 'PANDORA/', TOKEN_2022_PROGRAM_ID, BRIDGESPLIT_AUTHORITY) and the C2 path /cb/loopscale/* indicate targeting of Loopscale / Solana DeFi developers via a generic-scope dependency-confusion package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
